summary: Two-story rectangular Minecraft building with horizontally banded walls: lower and upper stonebrick/cobblestone layers separated by wooden plank and white slab bands, long narrow windows on both floors, a stepped wooden gable roof along the long axis, and a low white slab border/raised foundation around the base.

shape.overall_form: simple elongated rectangle with slightly inset short ends and a centered ridge roof
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the long axis (mirrored left-right); front/back appear similar

materials.name: cobblestone
materials.role: corner vertical accents / lower wall texture

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: white slab border / floor foundation (likely quartz or smooth stone slab)

elements: two floors with continuous horizontal window strips on each floor
stepped gable roof along the long axis made of wood planks with stone slab accents at the ridge ends
white slab low perimeter foundation/border around the building
narrow rectangular window openings (multiple panes) centered in each wall band
rebuild_hints: Base: place a white slab (quartz/smooth slab) perimeter 1 block high around the building footprint before wall construction.
Walls: build two stacked bands per story: lower cobblestone/stonebrick band with windows, then a horizontal wooden plank band, then a white slab band, repeat for second floor.
Windows: continuous 1-high rows of glass panes (several windows separated by single stonebrick posts) across long sides at each story level.
Roof: construct a gradual stepped wooden gable roof along the long axis using wood planks; add stone slab/stonebrick accents at the short-end steps and a small central cap at ridge.
Corners: use cobblestone blocks at vertical corners to create textured corner posts.
uncertainties: Exact block types for the white slab/foundation: looks like quartz slab but could be smooth stone slab; labeled slab_stone as likely.
Distinction between stonebrick and another gray masonry for the mid-wall band: appears like stonebrick but could be another variant.
Precise interior height and floor thickness not visible; floors_estimate assumes single block-per-floor wall height as visible windows.
Exact footprint dimensions (width/depth) are estimated from pixel views and top-down images; may vary by ±1-2 blocks.
Whether there is an entrance on the unseen side or integrated into one of the long faces is unknown (no door visible).
